name: LAUNCH DECK - SaaS dark metric hero
ref: Enerblock (BANILA Studio + Workoholics, Awwwards SOTD May 17 2026, Developer Award, GSAP + Astro); Linear product landing dark-mode; Serve Robotics SOTD June 12 2026
palette: #0C0B11 void + #FF5949 danger-red accent + bone #F4F1EB type; metric counters in amber #E8C547
brief: A SaaS/product LANDING-AND-CONVERSION page, the first in the library: full-viewport dark hero with three live-counting stat widgets (users, uptime %, requests served) ticking up on load, a one-line headline, a primary CTA + secondary demo link; below, a three-column feature bento in the same dark shell, then a pale pricing anchor. It carries the structural DNA of a real product launch (hero intent -> feature proof -> conversion path) rather than a media gallery with a hero. Grounded in Enerblock's #FF5949-on-#0C0B11 GSAP micro-interactions and Serve Robotics' high-performance framing. Mobile stacks the stat widgets vertically above the CTA. Pure CSS counter animation (@keyframes + counter-increment), no heavy JS, no WebGL. CAPTURE scroll=0: dark void fill, three amber counter widgets rendered as large monospace numbers mid-increment, a single oversized headline, two CTAs - the stat-ticker mechanic is legible as a static frame.

name: BIO CARD - link-in-bio glass stack
ref: Apple Liquid Glass (WWDC 2026); creator-economy link-in-bio design 2026 (embedsocial, taap.bio); Elva (Awwwards SOTD June 15 2026, Developer Award)
palette: #0D0D10 void with a slow-drift conic gradient aura behind + ice-white glass panels; avatar ring in a spinnable oklch hue; link accent tints per destination type
brief: The ONLY creator-hub page type in the library: a phone-first link-in-bio built as a vertical glass stack. Circular avatar at top with an animated oklch hue ring, a display-name headline, a one-line tagline, then 4-7 frosted-glass link tiles stacked below. Each tile has an icon, label, and on hover a real URL preview card (og:image + domain) anchored below it via CSS anchor positioning (Baseline 2026). A soft ambient gradient behind the stack drifts on a slow rAF. Distinct from liquid-glass-depth, which is a media GALLERY with depth parallax, not an avatar-first single-column link aggregator. Mobile is the primary viewport by definition; no WebGL. CAPTURE scroll=0: spinning oklch avatar ring, display name, tagline, and 4-5 frosted-glass link tiles - instantly recognizable as link-in-bio; the URL preview card is the only capture-harder element and is not load-bearing for the archetype read.

name: COMPARE - spec / pricing comparison as the page
ref: Vercel pricing SOTD; Linear pricing 2025 (Godly); Stripe pricing (SaaS Design Awards 2026); Levels.fyi comparison (Awwwards SOTD 2025)
palette: #ffffff + #0f0f0f ink + #2c6fad primary-column highlight + #e8f0ff tinted recommended column + #E8C547 'Popular' badge + one lime #C2F53E CTA
brief: The entire page IS a premium three-column spec/pricing comparison table treated as the design object (folds in the pricing-toggle idea): Free / Pro / Enterprise, giant column headers, a sticky feature-label column, animated check/x marks that draw in on first scroll, a highlighted recommended column with a subtle card-lift shadow, a 'Most Popular' badge, an annual/monthly toggle that morphs the price numbers via CSS @property, and a FAQ accordion at the bottom, with a CTA row pinned to the viewport bottom. The first table-native, conversion-native layout in the library; no built template uses a data table as the architectural backbone (blueprint-svg only annotates images). Mobile collapses to a single-column accordion where each feature row reveals all tier values on tap. Pure HTML table + CSS transitions, no canvas. CAPTURE scroll=0: three columns fully visible with headers, five feature rows of check/x icons, the middle column tinted blue with a lift shadow, the toggle and lime CTA visible - reads as a pricing page immediately without the toggle interaction.

name: CHAIN - agentic reasoning trace as gallery
ref: Anthropic Claude extended-thinking UI 2026; Vercel v0 tool-call trace; Devin AI task trace; OpenAI o3 reasoning display
palette: #0f1117 deep navy + #f0ece4 bone + #00d4ff cyan tool-call accent + #e8c547 amber thinking-step + #3a3a4a collapsed-step grey
brief: Each work is revealed through an agentic reasoning trace: collapsed grey step pills (plan, search, tool_call) expand in sequence until a final step shows the generated image resolving inside a thin amber 'output' border; the gallery is a scrolling stack of these traces. Uses the specific visual grammar of modern AI reasoning UIs - collapsible steps, cyan-typed tool-call boxes with arguments and results, a thinking-time badge - which no built template has: stdout is flat CLI output, ops-dashboard is a metrics dashboard, generating is a prompt-to-result reel. Mobile keeps the same collapsed/expanded step pattern, narrower. CSS accordion + keyframe step animation, no WebGL. CAPTURE scroll=0: one expanded trace with 3 collapsed grey step pills above, one open step showing a cyan tool_call box with args + result, the final step showing the generated image with the amber output border, and an amber '3.2s' thinking-time badge - the agentic grammar reads in one static frame.

name: CONFIGURE - product configurator as hero
ref: Porsche Taycan configurator (Awwwards SOTM 2025); BMW i7 360 configurator; Snohetta configurator (CSS Design Awards 2026); Teenage Engineering product builder
palette: #f8f6f2 light matte + #1a1a1a ink + #e03c2c accent indicator + #c8c8c8 option ring
brief: The hero section IS a multi-option configurator with no image gallery in sight: a product silhouette at center, a ring of option pills (color/material/size/variant) along the bottom, and a live stat strip (price, weight, lead-time) that updates as options toggle. The first option-selection-driving-derived-state layout in the library; split-diptych is a before/after slider and skeuomorphic-mixer is media-as-fader, but neither derives state from selection. Mobile collapses the option ring to a horizontal scroll row. Pure CSS-variable updates, one IntersectionObserver entrance, no WebGL. CAPTURE scroll=0: the product occupies center viewport with three color swatches highlighted, the price readout showing the current total, and two option pills in a selected state - self-explanatory with zero interaction.

name: TRANSCRIPT - conversational voice thread as gallery
ref: ChatGPT voice mode UI 2026; Apple Siri visual transcript; Perplexity voice interface; Google NotebookLM podcast UI
palette: #f7f5f2 parchment + #111111 ink + #007aff message-blue + #e5e5ea grey reply bubbles + #ff3b30 waveform red
brief: The portfolio is a voice-conversation transcript: the visitor scrolls a chat thread where each exchange is a voice turn - a waveform bubble (blue assistant, grey user), a transcript line below it, and works appear as attachment cards inside the assistant turns. Navigating the gallery means reading a conversation about the work. Uses the specific iOS/ChatGPT voice-thread grammar (waveform blobs, timestamps, attachment previews) that nothing built touches; query is a cursor-reveal index, manifesto-essay is a long-read. Phone-native, strongest on mobile. Pure CSS flex bubbles, seeded static waveform SVGs, no audio required, no WebGL. CAPTURE scroll=0: two complete exchanges - a grey user bubble with waveform + transcript, then a blue assistant bubble with waveform + transcript + a 2x2 grid of thumbnail attachment cards - plus a third grey user bubble mid-transcribing with a blinking cursor; the conversational grammar is unambiguous in one frame.

name: STREAM - token-cursor streaming generation canvas
ref: Anthropic extended-thinking UI 2026; Vercel v0 streaming response; OpenAI streaming completions visual language
palette: #0a0a0c near-black + #f0ece4 bone type + #e8472a blinking token cursor + #2c6fad ghost-incomplete tint
brief: The gallery is structured as an LLM response being streamed in real time: each work appears as a paragraph materializing token by token with a blinking red cursor, ghost-incomplete text fading in from the right, and the media resolving from a blurred placeholder into the full image as its generation completes - the incompleteness IS the brand. Distinct from generating (which shows prompt-to-result as finished beats) and stdout (CLI metaphor): this is the in-progress streaming UX itself as art direction. Mobile narrows the column, same token stream. Pure CSS typewriter + CSS blur-to-sharp transition, no WebGL. CAPTURE scroll=0 (seed mid-stream): two paragraphs already complete with images resolved, a third paragraph ~60% typed with a ghost tail fading right, a blinking cursor, and the third image still blurred at 8px with a pulsing shimmer - the streaming state is legible immediately.

name: INVOKE - keyboard command-palette portfolio
ref: Command-palette pattern (Linear, Vercel, Raycast 2025); SaaS UI Design Trends 2026 (saasui.design); Awwwards developer portfolios 2025-2026
palette: #0f0f12 void + #f0ece4 bone + #3a7bd5 cobalt active-match highlight + #e8c547 amber shortcut badge
brief: The portfolio IS a functional command palette. A full-viewport typographic index sits behind a centered modal that opens on load (or Cmd+K); typing filters the index in real time, arrow keys navigate, Enter zooms the selected work to full screen - works are navigable entirely without a mouse. Distinct from stdout (a brutalist print aesthetic with no real interactivity) and variable-font-pressure (kinetic type index): the palette modal is the primary navigation surface, not a secondary shortcut layer. Mobile gets a search-bar equivalent with tap selection. Zero WebGL. CAPTURE scroll=0: the command-palette modal open over a dim typographic list, cursor blinking in the search field, two or three amber shortcut badges visible and one filtered result highlighted in cobalt - the mechanic is fully readable from a static screenshot.

name: DATASAY - scrollytelling data story
ref: The Pudding 'Film Dialogue'; NYT Upshot scrollytelling; Amelia Wattenberger 'Scrollytelling'; Domestic Data Streamers (Awwwards SOTD 2025)
palette: #0f1117 near-black + #f5f2eb cream prose + #e8c547 amber highlight + #3a7bd5 chart-blue
brief: A sticky-panel data story: prose scrolls in a right column while a data chart in the left panel live-updates its encoding (bar width, dot position, color saturation) as each paragraph's scroll progress is read - actual data-driven chart geometry as the scroll-synchronized visual layer. Nothing built does this; scrollytelling-chapter uses a chapter rail and inline media, not live chart state. Mobile stacks the chart above each prose block. SVG paths + CSS custom properties, no canvas, no WebGL. CAPTURE scroll=0: the left panel shows a bar chart mid-transition with one bar highlighted amber and the right prose column frozen at the sentence that caused that highlight - the dual-column structure reads immediately as data journalism, not a portfolio.

name: LEDGER - persistent split master-detail
ref: SquareKicker split-screen sticky scrolling (Awwwards 2025); AVL Media Group sticky-sidebar pattern; iOS/macOS master-detail UX; Studeo Brand Agency sidebar layout (Awwwards)
palette: #f9f6f0 archival paper + #111 ink + #d4a84b amber left-rail accent; Newsreader italic + Space Mono
brief: The viewport is permanently split: a fixed left rail (30vw) lists all works as a numbered typographic ledger with metadata (date, medium, seed); the right panel scrolls independently through the full content of the selected work, cross-fading on click. The whole catalogue is readable in the left rail without touching the right panel. Distinct from scrollytelling-chapter (a dot nav, not a full persistent content list) and anchor-annotated-index (hover-annotation into the margin, not a persistent split). Mobile collapses the rail to a slide-up drawer. No WebGL. CAPTURE scroll=0: the permanent split - left rail with 6-8 numbered ledger rows, one amber-highlighted, right panel showing that work's full content - the master-detail structure is fully legible as a static frame.

name: SPOKE - radial hub portfolio
ref: Awwwards circular-navigation collection; radial/pie-menu UX pattern (Articulate 2025 radial-nav showcase); Awwwards unusual-navigation winners 2025
palette: #09090b void + #f4f0e8 bone spokes + #c94a2e active-sector fill
brief: Works are arranged as wedge sectors around a central hub on a fixed single-screen canvas; rotating the ring (drag, scroll wheel, or arrow keys) brings the next sector to the 12-o-clock position where it blooms to full detail. Nothing scrolls, no page changes, the full set is always visible as a ring - the radial structure IS the archetype. Distinct from particle-constellation (a flat star field with no directional structure) and infinite-canvas (free pan). Mobile collapses to a radial arc at the bottom of the screen. Pure CSS transforms + one rAF for rotation inertia, no WebGL. CAPTURE scroll=0: the full ring visible with one sector bloomed open at top and the others as labelled wedges fanning around - the radial structure is fully legible before any interaction.

name: CONTROL DECK - single-screen fixed gallery
ref: FWA no-scroll single-screen experiments; Lusion v3 fixed-grid navigation (Awwwards + FWA SOTY); Awwwards unusual-navigation 2025
palette: #0c0c0e near-black + #e8e0d0 warm bone + #c9794a copper active-cell ring
brief: All works are simultaneously visible as a fixed grid filling 100dvh with no scroll at all; clicking or arrowing through cells changes the focused cell (it expands in place with a copper ring and plays its media) while all others remain visible and dimmed at their natural sizes - the entire site is the homepage, no lazy load, no pagination, no detail pages. Distinct from ops-dashboard (data tables and charts as content) and surveillance-cctv (CCTV-monitor-wall aesthetic): this is the no-scroll control-deck paradigm. Mobile reduces to a 2-column fixed grid; full keyboard tab/arrow + focus ring. No WebGL. CAPTURE scroll=0: the entire site - a fixed grid of 6-9 media cells filling the viewport, one copper-ringed cell larger and playing, the rest equal-sized - the single-screen paradigm is visible with no scroll implied.

name: THE ROOM - cinematic single-environment scroll timeline
ref: GQ & AP 'The Extraordinary Lab' by Immersive Garden (Awwwards SOTM April 2026; CSSDA; Theater.js + Nuxt + Blender + WebGL)
palette: #0a0a10 deep interior + warm tungsten fill #c9844a + bone paper panel #f0ece4 + precision steel #8a9aaa; depth from lighting
brief: A single physical room or environment modeled in Blender and rendered in three.js whose camera travels a fixed spline as you scroll: the viewport is always inside one continuous space, different angles revealing different works, product details, or narrative chapters, every camera beat and light change authored to the scroll timeline. The ROOM IS the content, not a wrapper. Distinct from space (a fly-through of separate framed planes), corridor-walk (first-person ink-sketch on a straight axis), and scrollytelling-chapter (CSS/HTML editorial prose). This is the batch's primary WebGL slot (count 1 of ~2). Mobile falls back to a simplified 2D panel sequence with the same chapter beats. CAPTURE scroll=0: the camera opens on a dramatic wide interior shot of the 3D room with the hero work lit and visible - the room-as-world paradigm reads in one frame (open on the wide shot, not a black void).

name: METHOD - recipe / how-to step layout
ref: NYT Cooking visual recipe pages; Bon Appetit Test Kitchen web format; Serious Eats hierarchy; Cook Unity (Awwwards SOTD 2025)
palette: #fefcf8 warm white + #1a1614 ink + #c94a2e vermilion step number + #e8c547 timer badge
brief: Each step of a recipe or tutorial is a full-width horizontal band: a large numbered circle on the left, a Newsreader-italic instruction headline, a supporting detail paragraph, an optional inline ingredient-badge cluster, and an optional media thumbnail clipped to step height on the right; a sticky progress column on the left tracks 'you are on step N of M'. Nothing built structures content around sequential instructional steps with numbered blocks and a live step state. Mobile collapses the media thumbnail above each step block. IntersectionObserver only, no WebGL. CAPTURE scroll=0: steps 1 and 2 partially visible - large red circled numerals, bold serif instructions, and small ingredient-pill clusters - the instructional how-to hierarchy reads instantly even without reading the text.

name: MARKED UP - annotated case study
ref: Instrument case-study pages (Awwwards 2025-2026); Huge Inc work-detail pages; Koto Studio case studies (FWA 2025); Heco Partners format
palette: #faf8f4 paper + #111 ink + #d4a84b amber annotation marker + #e8472a vermilion callout line
brief: Each section is a case-study block with an image or mockup on one side and numbered annotation callouts (small circles with lines pointing to specific zones of the image) on the other, revealing on scroll, the callout numbers tethered to their targets via CSS anchor positioning (Baseline 2026). Structurally distinct from everything built: anchor-annotated-index annotates a list row, not specific pixels inside an image. Mobile hides the callout lines and shows numbered tabs beneath the image. CSS anchor-position + IntersectionObserver, no WebGL. CAPTURE scroll=0: a mockup screenshot fills the left half with three amber numbered circles overlaid on specific zones and matching callout panels anchored to the right with connector lines visible - the annotation grammar is instantly legible.

name: VIEWFINDER - AR camera framing as still portfolio
ref: Apple Vision Pro spatial UI 2026; Snap Lens Studio AR overlay; Teenage Engineering CM-15 viewfinder; Awwwards immersive-AR nominees 2026
palette: #000000 void + #00ff88 reticle green + #ffffff HUD type + translucent grid overlay; works appear inside detected bounding boxes
brief: A full-viewport AR camera viewfinder is the gallery interface: corner-bracket reticles, a focus grid, and each work 'detected' as an object with a bounding box, confidence score, and category label in green HUD type, as if the camera is scanning a physical installation. Distinct from surveillance-cctv (a monitor wall) and ops-dashboard (data metrics): this is the mobile AR scanning aesthetic, not broadcast or ops. Mobile is identical and actually more native. Pure CSS + SVG overlays, zero canvas, no WebGL. CAPTURE scroll=0: a viewfinder with corner brackets, a 3x3 focus grid, and three detected bounding boxes with green labels (SUBJECT_01 98.4%, SCENE_02 91.7%, ARTIFACT_03 87.2%) each containing a real work image, plus a top HUD with a REC dot - reads immediately as a static frame.

name: TERRAIN NAV - drag-to-explore 3D topographic map
ref: San Rita studio (Codrops 'Digital Craft, Wild Soul' Mar 2026 + 'No Two Paths Alike' Jun 2026; Awwwards 'Mapping the Uncharted')
palette: #0f1923 cartographic navy + sun-faded ochre terrain #b8a070 + waypoint amber #c9a35b + parchment panel #f0e8d4
brief: The entire portfolio lives on a top-down 3D terrain generated from a real heightmap, dragged and zoomed like a hiking map; each project is a waypoint marker that, when clicked, zooms the camera into a terrain detail that unfolds into a work panel. Critically distinct from svgmap-journey (a 2D SVG path that draws itself on scroll along a fixed route): this is a FREE-PAN 3D terrain explored non-linearly, more spatial-board than scroll narrative. The batch's second WebGL slot (count 2 of ~2). Mobile uses pinch-zoom + drag touch events. three.js terrain mesh. CAPTURE (moderately hard): seed the scene with a pre-positioned birds-eye terrain view already centered, waypoints glowing, and one project panel half-unfolded to the side so the paradigm is legible at scroll=0; a gentle auto-rotate on load confirms the 3D scene is live.

name: ONBOARD - multi-step form / quiz flow
ref: Typeform visual form design (Awwwards 2024-2025); Notion onboarding SOTD; Linear onboarding (Godly 2026); Mailchimp onboarding award 2025
palette: #f5f2ed off-white + #111 ink + #2c6fad step-active blue + #e8c547 progress amber
brief: A full-viewport multi-step form/quiz where each step occupies the full viewport and transitions via a vertical slide-up (one step per screen), a sticky progress indicator at top tracks completion as a filled arc per step, and the final step is a summary card. Structurally zero overlap with anything built: every other template treats media assets as payload; here the payload IS the form fields and response state (onboarding, surveys, qualification wizards, product finders). Mobile is identical since the layout is single-column by design. CSS transition on transform:translateY per step, no canvas, no WebGL. CAPTURE scroll=0: the first question fills the viewport with a large typographic headline, three option buttons below, and a five-dot progress arc at top with dot 1 filled - the form structure reads at a glance with zero interaction.

name: ON AIR - live broadcast reaction overlay gallery
ref: Twitch live-reaction UI 2026; YouTube SuperChat live premiere; Moment Factory live event broadcast; NGR.EV broadcast aesthetic (Awwwards 2026)
palette: #0d0d0f near-black + #ff0000 LIVE dot + #ffffff type + per-emoji reaction colors; works are the stream content
brief: The gallery is framed as a live video broadcast watched with an audience: works cycle on a broadcast frame with a LIVE dot and viewer count, seeded emoji reactions float upward on the right rail, and a frozen chat feed shows audience comments about the work. Distinct from real-time-collab-cursors (cursor presence, design-review metaphor): this is the asymmetric livestream consumption metaphor (one performer, many watchers) with reactions and chat as the social layer. Mobile stacks the chat rail below the broadcast frame. CSS animation for floating reactions, no WebGL. CAPTURE scroll=0: a full-bleed work in the broadcast frame with a red LIVE dot + viewer count top-left, five emoji reactions (fire, eyes, heart, 100, sparkle) mid-float at various heights on the right edge, and a semi-transparent chat rail with 4-5 seeded color-coded messages - the broadcast grammar is instantly legible.

name: EPOCH - interactive chronological timeline
ref: Basement Systems timeline (Awwwards SOTD 2025); Knight Lab TimelineJS; NASA history timeline (CSS Design Awards 2025); Reuters Graphics Moments 2024
palette: #0c0c10 near-black + #e8c547 amber active node + #f0ece4 bone text + #2c6fad blue era-band
brief: A fixed vertical spine runs the full page height with era nodes on it; clicking a node jumps to that era's full-width content panel and scroll advances a glowing amber progress dot along the spine, with eras color-banded. The spine itself is a data object with dates, eras, and jumps. Distinct from playhead (a horizontal media scrubber) and scrollytelling-chapter (a chapter-dot reading index): neither is a chronological timeline. Mobile rotates the spine to a horizontal scrollable ticker at the top. IntersectionObserver + CSS clip-path for the spine fill, no WebGL. CAPTURE scroll=0: the full vertical spine visible on the left with four era nodes, the amber dot at node 1, and the first era's full-width editorial panel filling the right two-thirds - the timeline grammar reads without scrolling.

name: FIGURES - stat-driven annual report
ref: Mailchimp Annual Report 2024 (Awwwards SOTM); Dropbox annual report editorial 2025; Stripe annual letter; N26 transparency report (CSS Design Awards 2025); Spotify Wrapped layout (FWA 2024)
palette: #f0ece4 bone + #111 ink + #3a7bd5 metric-blue + #e8c547 amber callout + #c94a2e negative-delta red
brief: A full-page vertical annual report: a hero stat block where the number itself is the hero (Fraunces display at 20vw), then alternating stat+context sections where a counter animates from 0 on IntersectionObserver entry, each with a supporting sparkline SVG and a one-line human context phrase. The opposite of ops-dashboard (a CI/CD control-room metaphor for a technical audience): this is editorial, human, brand-facing, with narrative context per stat. Distinct from saas-dark-metric-hero (a dark conversion-funnel landing) - this is a light editorial report whose whole body is figures. Mobile is identical (single-column). SVG sparklines + requestAnimationFrame counters, no WebGL. CAPTURE scroll=0: a single enormous number (e.g. '1.4M') in display serif occupies most of the viewport with a small amber label above and a one-line context sentence below - the stat-as-hero annual-report structure is unmistakable.

name: EXTRA - broadsheet newspaper column grid
ref: Le Monde digital redesign 2024 (Awwwards honorable mention); The Athletic editorial (Webby 2025); NYT T Magazine interactive 2025; OFF+BRAND manifesto (Awwwards SOTD); Study Hall Design Studio (Awwwards SOTD 2026)
palette: #faf8f2 newsprint + #111111 ink + #c8102e masthead red + #e5e0d8 column-rule gray; Fraunces + Space Mono bylines
brief: A dense multi-column newspaper broadsheet: justified body type in a strict 5-6 col grid, headline hierarchy tiers (display 96pt / deck 36pt / body 11pt), horizontal column rules, a spanning masthead, a folio, and a sidebar 'Facts & Numbers' stat column - information density and editorial authority. Doubles as the library's first about/team page type when each entry is a person with a date byline and a one-line 'beat'. Distinct from magazine-takeover (a glossy cover page with masthead + cover lines + barcode) and manifesto-essay (a literary single-column long-read with drop-caps and footnotes): this is the BROADSHEET register, justified column measure. Mobile collapses to a single honest column with the density intact. Zero JS, pure CSS grid + columns, no WebGL. CAPTURE scroll=0: the full newspaper grid, masthead, column rules, and headline hierarchy all visible - reads immediately as a broadsheet.

name: STORY - fashion editorial interior spread
ref: AnOther Magazine interactive 2025 (FWA FOTM); i-D Digital 2025 (CSS Design Awards); Dazed & Confused digital spreads 2025; Wonderland editorial nav
palette: #f9f6f2 spread paper + #0a0a0a ink; one per-story accent for captions (dusty rose #c8a095 or slate #6b7a8d); Playfair Display / Cormorant Garamond + Helvetica Neue caption
brief: A full-bleed interior editorial-spread sequence: each scroll step is one magazine spread with a full-bleed portrait on the left and styled copy (story title, kicker, caption, folio number) set small in the lower-right corner - no header, no navigation, no branding, just the spread. This is the fashion editorial grammar SOTY editorial winners use, and it is NOT magazine-takeover (the cover page with masthead, cover lines, barcode). Mobile stacks to a single portrait with caption below. No WebGL. CAPTURE scroll=0: a full-bleed portrait with the minimal caption block bottom-right reads as a fashion spread instantly, no interaction needed.

name: RAPPORT - silent-luxury restraint
ref: Koto Studio annual-report microsites 2025 (CSS Design Awards); Bottega Veneta digital editorial 2024-2025; Maison Margiela brand site (Awwwards nominee 2025)
palette: #fdfcfa near-white + #0e0e0e near-black; NO accent color - hierarchy from scale and weight alone; EB Garamond or Cormorant at large scale
brief: Extreme negative space, a single high-quality Garamond-class serif, hierarchy expressed only through size and weight (no color, no rule, no decoration), and one work per scroll panel occupying ~60% of the viewport with a brief caption line. The restraint IS the art direction - what luxury brands do when they have nothing to prove. Absent from the library: the closest, manifesto-essay, has drop caps, vermilion accents, pull-quotes and footnotes. Mobile keeps the white space and drops to a single centered column, no scroll-jacking. No WebGL. The one risk: it will look empty unless the type scale and proportion are treated with real craft. CAPTURE scroll=0: an oversized Garamond headline on white ground with generous leading and one image reads as instantly distinct from every other template.

name: AQUA - Frutiger Aero tech-optimism revival
ref: Frutiger Aero revival (Awwwards trend report 2025-2026); Design Milk Y2K-to-Aero feature 2025; itsnicethat.com Aero-revival pieces 2025
palette: #e8f4fd sky blue + #ffffff + #9fd4f5 gloss highlight + #5ba8d5 + #3d7a3d nature green; bevel, emboss, gel buttons
brief: The 2004-2010 tech-optimism aesthetic fully committed: sky-blue gradients, glossy pill buttons with bevel/emboss, a rounded Frutiger-style sans (Nunito or Varela), nature motifs (leaves, water, clouds), gel-badge UI, and soft drop shadows - shown without irony. Works sit inside glossy beveled frames like desktop widgets. Distinct from retro-futurism-holo (void-black Y3K iridescence) and interface-nostalgia-os (flat Win95 pixel + taskbar): Aero is digital optimism, softness, and nature, a documented 2025-2026 revival. Mobile should feel like a 2007 mobile web UI built with 2026 CSS. No WebGL. CAPTURE scroll=0: sky-blue gradient, glossy pill UI, a bevel-bordered work frame, and gel buttons all visible immediately, no scroll needed.

name: DIFF LOG - changelog release-notes feed
ref: Linear changelog (linear.app/changelog, 'Introducing Linear Agent' Mar 2026); Linear 'Now' page; 15 SaaS Changelog UI examples 2026 (saasframe.io)
palette: #0D0D10 void + bone #F0ECE4 type + category pills: feature cobalt #2C6FAD, fix amber #E8C547, breaking vermilion #C0392B; Space Mono + Inter
brief: A vertical changelog feed where each entry is a card with a version badge (v2.4.1), a date, category pills (Feature / Fix / Breaking), a one-line headline, a body paragraph, and an optional CSS-only syntax-highlighted diff block for code-adjacent changes; the hero at scroll=0 is the most recent entry rendered oversized as a 'latest release' spotlight with a lime pulse on the version badge, and a sticky left sidebar shows a year/month tree. The first page type whose structural unit is a time-ordered VERSION ENTRY rather than a media work; distinct from interactive-timeline (an era spine) - this is a release-communication feed. Mobile hides the sidebar and uses a compact pill-only date header. No WebGL. CAPTURE scroll=0: the 'latest release' spotlight card dominates - large pulsing version badge, category pills, headline, two-line body - the changelog purpose is immediately legible because the version badge and date are architectural.

name: MAGNIFY - semantic-zoom content hierarchy
ref: Windows Phone semantic zoom (UX precedent); Figma zoom-level UX; 'LEVEL' (Awwwards SOTD 2026); Codrops scroll-zoom experiments 2025
palette: #121214 near-black + #e8e4dc warm bone + #00c896 teal zoom-level indicator ring
brief: Three content levels navigated by scroll-to-zoom or pinch: Level 1 = a thumbnail grid of all works (overview), Level 2 = a single work card with metadata (selection), Level 3 = a full-bleed immersive detail with prompt, process notes, media. Scrolling down zooms in one level, up zooms out, via a CSS scale + opacity morph with no page navigation; a teal ring and a persistent breadcrumb (L1 > L2 > L3) show position. Distinct from infinite-canvas (pan a 2D board, no content hierarchy) and view-transition-morph (click-to-detail, two levels, no zoom metaphor). Mobile pinch-to-zoom triggers the same transitions. No WebGL. CAPTURE scroll=0: Level 1 - a compact thumbnail grid, one card mid-zoom-in with a teal ring growing around it, and the breadcrumb visible in the corner - the zoom-level concept reads from the static thumbnail.

name: THE VAULT - studio culture capsule / archive
ref: Better Off Studio 'Lookback' by Gil Huybrecht & Jesper Landberg (Codrops Mar 2026; betteroff.studio)
palette: #f2ede4 archival paper + ink #1a1710 + warm dust #c9a35b year-numeral + slate-violet #6a5a8a tag pills; faint linen grain
brief: A 'what shaped us' digital time-capsule: a masonry grid of pinned cultural artifacts (books, screenshots, sketches, films, music tracks, events) each tagged by year and influence type, scroll-revealed with a gentle stagger, clickable to a full-screen exhibit. NOT a portfolio (no client work), NOT a manifesto essay (no long prose column), NOT a bento gallery (no per-modality accent system): a curated studio bibliography presented as a museum reading room. Mobile is a single-column vertical scroll with a floating year-index. Zero WebGL, pure CSS + IntersectionObserver. CAPTURE scroll=0: an above-the-fold 3x4 masonry of artifact tiles in varying proportions, a large year numeral, and a floating filter ribbon - no scroll required to read the paradigm.

name: DECK - horizontal chaptered presentation
ref: Patrick Heng portfolio (Awwwards SOTD 2025, horizontal-scroll-navigation); Awwwards horizontal-layout collection; Igloo.inc horizontal editorial
palette: #f5f1eb warm parchment + #1c1814 ink + #2c6fad cobalt chapter-tab; Fraunces + Space Grotesk
brief: Works are chapters in a horizontal-only presentation deck: a persistent chapter-tab strip runs along the top (like a presentation toolbar), clicking jumps to a chapter, left/right arrows or swipe navigate, and each chapter is a full-viewport horizontal panel with a distinct layout (title spread, diptych, full bleed, captions). Unlike axis-shift-scroll (which pivots from vertical to horizontal mid-scroll) this is horizontal from the first pixel with no vertical component; unlike playhead (a scrub timeline) the chapters are named and editorially distinct. Mobile uses swipe-snap and the chapter strip collapses to a dot indicator. No WebGL. CAPTURE scroll=0: the first chapter panel fills the viewport, the cobalt chapter-tab strip across the top shows 4-5 named tabs with tab 1 active-underlined, and a right-arrow hint sits at the viewport edge - the horizontal deck structure reads before any swipe.

name: LA CARTA - restaurant character scroll
ref: La Revoltosa (WAKA, Awwwards SOTD May 21 2026, Developer Award); Crav Burgers (Anyflow, Awwwards SOTD June 13 2026, Developer Award)
palette: #F91814 brand red (or deep terracotta #2C1810) + #F5E3CD cream + ink #1A1008; all food color from photography
brief: A restaurant/food-brand landing built around ONE illustrated brand character fixed in a left panel while the right panel scrolls through hero headline, menu-category teaser, a reservation CTA, and a story section; the character's pose shifts per scroll section (idle, pointing at the menu, raising a glass, waving goodbye) via CSS clip-path sprite swap, no WebGL. Purpose-built for hospitality conversion - character-as-spokesperson + menu teaser + book-a-table CTA are the structural bones - which folds in the persistent-character idea while staying a real page TYPE, not a nav gimmick. Grounded in La Revoltosa's award-winning 'character does the selling' approach. Mobile collapses to a stacked full-bleed hero with the character above each scroll section. CAPTURE scroll=0: a saturated full-bleed color field, a large illustrated character figure left, hero headline right, brand name, and a 'Reserve' CTA - the character-as-brand-identity restaurant page reads immediately as a static screenshot.

name: RAW - brutalist default-browser utility register
ref: Cargo Collective minimal portfolio tier (Awwwards 2025); brutalistwebsites.com curatorial features; Are.na design approach; Hito Steyerl website aesthetic
palette: #ffffff + #000000 + hyperlink #0000ee / visited #551a8b; Times New Roman or Courier; no custom type, no custom color
brief: Absolute refusal of design as a design move: default system fonts (Times New Roman or Courier), hyperlink blue, thin black horizontal rules, pure information density, no padding, no color. Works are plain HTML image elements with alt-text captions and a text index, resembling an early-web academic or art-institution page. Distinct from stdout (a dark CLI aesthetic with custom phosphor colors and a heavily styled terminal frame): this is RAW HTML brutalism that deliberately uses DEFAULT browser rendering as the aesthetic - the anti-style is the style. Naturally responsive on mobile (a single column of unstyled text and images). No WebGL. CAPTURE scroll=0: Times New Roman, blue links, horizontal rules, and an image with alt caption visible immediately as the default-browser aesthetic, no interaction needed.

name: PRESS RUN - seriograph flat spot-color print
ref: Aesthetic Apparatus silkscreen poster design (Awwwards print-revival 2025); Saul Bass centenary editorial 2026; AIGA Eye on Design print-to-web; Methane Studios
palette: #f2ede3 off-white stock + flat spot colors only: #e8421a vermilion + #1a3566 navy + #f5c842 process yellow; NO gradients, NO halftones
brief: A flat silkscreen/seriograph aesthetic: solid opaque ink layers, paper-stock texture, bold graphic shapes, no gradients, no halftones. Each work is a flat graphic screen-print poster panel with simplified shapes, registration-offset layers, and tactile paper grain - the Saul Bass tradition applied to digital: geometric, iconic, flat. Distinct from risograph-print-emulation (two-color Bayer halftone dots with misregistration shimmer): seriograph is flat ink, graphic, no dots. Mobile works as-is since it is a flat non-JS layout. No WebGL. CAPTURE scroll=0: flat opaque color layers, visible paper grain, and bold graphic type with no gradients read instantly as screen-print, not risograph.
